question.primary_nl: Find the tail number and origin of all flights that were canceled due to weather on January 15, 2016.
nl_variants: Which aircraft tail numbers and origin airports had flights canceled for weather reasons on 2016-01-15?
Show me the origins and tailnums for flights with a cancellation code of 'Weather' on Jan 15, 2016.
Weather cancellations on Jan 15: list tail numbers and origins.

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT t.tailnum, t.origin FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 t JOIN l_cancellation c ON t.cancellationcode = c.code WHERE c.description = 'Weather' AND t.flightdate = '2016-01-15';
evidence.reasoning_steps: Filters the fact table for the specific date '2016-01-15', joins with the l_cancellation table to filter by description 'Weather', and retrieves the requested string columns.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: On January 15, 2016, a total of 16 flights were canceled due to weather. Impacted aircraft included tail numbers such as N3749D (from JNU), N14162 (from ATW), N861AS (from ATL), N13989 (from CWA), and N642NK (from CLE).

nl_answer_variants.text: 16 weather-related cancellations occurred on Jan 15, 2016, including tail numbers N3749D, N14162, and N861AS.
Data analysis for January 15, 2016, indicates 16 cancellations specifically attributed to weather conditions. Sample origins included JNU, ATW, and ATL.
There were 16 flights canceled because of the weather on January 15. For example, planes flying out of JNU (N3749D) and ATW (N14162) didn't make it off the ground.

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT t.flightnum, t.destcityname FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 t JOIN l_weekdays w ON t.dayofweek = w.code WHERE t.carrier = 'WN' AND t.origin = 'ATL' AND w.description = 'Monday' AND t.flightdate < '2016-01-14';
evidence.reasoning_steps: Filters on string columns for carrier and origin, joins with l_weekdays to filter on 'Monday', and applies a strictly less-than date filter based on the reference time.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Before January 14, 2016, Southwest Airlines (WN) operated 245 flights from ATL on Mondays. These included flights to destinations such as Austin, TX (Flight 193, 2712) and Boston, MA (Flight 806, 2577, 3356).

question.primary_nl: List the top 3 destination airports with the highest average taxi-in time for flights arriving between January 10 and January 20, 2016.

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT dest FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 WHERE flightdate BETWEEN '2016-01-10' AND '2016-01-20' GROUP BY dest ORDER BY AVG(taxiin) DESC LIMIT 3;
evidence.reasoning_steps: Aggregates the average of taxiin, groups by destination code, sorts descending, and limits to the top 3 results within the 11-day window.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Between January 10 and 20, 2016, the top three destination airports with the highest average taxi-in times were Chicago O'Hare (ORD), Los Angeles (LAX), and Miami (MIA).

question.primary_nl: Which airlines flew from ORD to LGA in January 2016 but did not have any flights on that route canceled due to the 'National Air System'?
nl_variants: Return the unique carrier descriptions for airlines traveling ORD to LGA in Jan 2016, EXCEPT those with a cancellation description of 'National Air System'.
List airlines operating between ORD and LGA in Jan 2016 that recorded absolutely zero 'National Air System' cancellations for that specific route.
Airlines flying ORD-LGA in Jan 2016 without any National Air System cancellations.

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT u.description FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 t JOIN l_unique_carriers u ON t.uniquecarrier = u.code WHERE t.origin = 'ORD' AND t.dest = 'LGA' AND t.flightdate BETWEEN '2016-01-01' AND '2016-01-31' EXCEPT SELECT u.description FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 t JOIN l_unique_carriers u ON t.uniquecarrier = u.code JOIN l_cancellation c ON t.cancellationcode = c.code WHERE t.origin = 'ORD' AND t.dest = 'LGA' AND c.description = 'National Air System' AND t.flightdate BETWEEN '2016-01-01' AND '2016-01-31';
evidence.reasoning_steps: Uses the EXCEPT operator to subtract the set of carriers with the specific cancellation reason on the specific route from the set of all carriers operating on that route over the entire month.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: The airlines that flew from ORD to LGA in January 2016 without any 'National Air System' cancellations were United Air Lines Inc., SkyWest Airlines Inc., American Airlines Inc., and Spirit Air Lines.

nl_answer_variants.text: United, SkyWest, American, and Spirit had zero NAS cancellations on the ORD-LGA route in Jan.
For the ORD-LGA sector in January 2016, United, SkyWest, American, and Spirit maintained operations without any cancellations attributed to the National Air System.
United, SkyWest, American, and Spirit all managed to fly from ORD to LGA in January without a single NAS-related cancellation.

evidence.reasoning_steps: Employs the INTERSECT operation to find the overlap of destination city market descriptions served by two distinct origin airports on a specific day.

question.primary_nl: Identify the carriers that operated flights in the '2500 Miles and Greater' distance group and also had at least one flight with a carrier delay greater than 60 minutes, during the period of January 25 to January 31, 2016.
nl_variants: Which unique carrier descriptions had distancegroup '2500 Miles and Greater' INTERSECT carriers with carrierdelay > 60 between 2016-01-25 and 2016-01-31?

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT u.description FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 t JOIN l_unique_carriers u ON t.uniquecarrier = u.code JOIN l_distance_group_250 d ON t.distancegroup = d.code WHERE d.description = '2500 Miles and Greater' AND t.flightdate BETWEEN '2016-01-25' AND '2016-01-31' INTERSECT SELECT u.description FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 t JOIN l_unique_carriers u ON t.uniquecarrier = u.code WHERE t.carrierdelay > 60 AND t.flightdate BETWEEN '2016-01-25' AND '2016-01-31';
evidence.reasoning_steps: Uses INTERSECT to enforce logical coexistence of two distinct conditions (long distance group and high carrier delay) applied to the carrier entity during the last week of the month.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: Between January 25 and 31, 2016, 7 carriers operated flights in the '2500 Miles and Greater' group and had at least one carrier delay over 60 minutes, including Virgin America, Delta, JetBlue, United, and Hawaiian.

nl_answer_variants.text: 7 long-haul carriers (including Delta and United) had 60+ min delays in the last week of Jan.
During the final week of January 2016, seven carriers involved in long-distance operations (≥ 2500 miles) recorded at least one carrier-attributed delay exceeding 60 minutes.

Find the destination codes that received United flights from SFO on Jan 10, 2016, ensuring Delta completely bypassed those same destinations from SFO that day.
Destinations served from SFO by UA but not DL on Jan 10.

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT dest FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 WHERE carrier = 'UA' AND origin = 'SFO' AND flightdate = '2016-01-10' EXCEPT SELECT dest FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 WHERE carrier = 'DL' AND origin = 'SFO' AND flightdate = '2016-01-10';
evidence.reasoning_steps: Applies an EXCEPT operator to calculate the set difference between the destination networks of two major competing carriers departing from the same hub on a single day.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: On January 10, 2016, United Airlines (UA) served destinations from SFO that Delta Air Lines (DL) did not, including PHX, LAS, DFW, RDU, and EWR.

nl_answer_variants.text: Dests served from SFO by UA but not DL on Jan 10: PHX, LAS, DFW, RDU, EWR.
A comparative analysis of SFO departures on Jan 10, 2016, identifies PHX, LAS, DFW, RDU, and EWR as destination codes unique to United Airlines (UA) relative to Delta Air Lines (DL).
On January 10th, United flew to PHX, LAS, and DFW from San Francisco, while Delta didn't have any flights to those same spots from SFO that day.

Find the destination codes that received United flights from LAX on Jan 12, 2016, ensuring American Airlines completely bypassed those same destinations from LAX that day.
Destinations served from LAX by UA but not AA on Jan 12.

evidence.golden_sql: SELECT dest FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 WHERE carrier = 'UA' AND origin = 'LAX' AND flightdate = '2016-01-12' EXCEPT SELECT dest FROM on_time_on_time_performance_2016_1 WHERE carrier = 'AA' AND origin = 'LAX' AND flightdate = '2016-01-12';
evidence.reasoning_steps: Applies an EXCEPT operator to calculate the set difference between the destination networks of two major competing carriers departing from the same hub on a single day.

ground_truth.formatted_nl_answer: On January 12, 2016, United Airlines (UA) served 6 destination airports from LAX that were not served by American Airlines (AA), including MSY, DEN, IAH, EWR, and CLE.

nl_answer_variants.text: 6 airports (e.g., MSY, DEN, IAH) were served by UA but not AA from LAX on Jan 12.

A comparative network analysis at LAX for January 12, 2016, identifies a set of six destinations unique to United Airlines (UA) relative to American Airlines (AA), including New Orleans (MSY) and Denver (DEN).
On January 12th, United flew to six cities from LAX that American didn't, like New Orleans, Denver, and Houston.
